item.title: The Job Market May Be Better Than Workers Realize
item.description: We’ll find out more on Friday.

item.title: DoubleLine’s Cohen Says AI Bubble Is Coming to Credit Markets
item.description: Artificial intelligence debt will almost certainly reach bubble levels eventually, given the history of periods of heavy investments in areas like railroads and the internet, said DoubleLine portfolio manager Robert Cohen.

item.title: Fed’s Beige Book Shows Steady Employment, Higher Inflation
item.description: Employment remained stable in recent weeks as inflation continued to rise across much of the country, driven primarily by the impact of war in the Middle East on energy prices, the Federal Reserve said.

item.title: OpenAI CEO to Share Oversight Ideas in Wake of Trump AI Order
item.description: OpenAI Chief Executive Officer Sam Altman will push his vision for public-private collaboration on artificial intelligence during a visit to Washington on Wednesday, part of a broader policy agenda that also includes creating a vehicle to pass AI’s potentially vast financial windfall to consumers.

item.title: Duke CEO Sees AI Fueling Power Growth at 10 Times Historic Pace
item.description: Duke Energy Corp.’s chief executive officer is forecasting power demand will grow at 10 times the rate over the past several decades from the rise of AI data centers and the electrification of the economy.

item.title: Bessent Invokes Language on Inflation That Caused Yellen Trouble
item.description: Whether he calls it a “blip,” “transitory” or “transient,” Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent has taken to describing inflation in a way that came back to haunt his predecessor and the previous administration.

item.title: GoldenTree’s Tananbaum Eyes Credit-Equity Disconnects in Telecom
item.description: Distressed opportunities are emerging in sectors facing fundamental business-model challenges, including software and cable, with some of the most compelling situations arising from mismatches between debt and equity valuations, according to GoldenTree Asset Management founder Steven Tananbaum.

item.title: Anthropic Picks Morgan Stanley, Goldman Sachs to Lead IPO
item.description: Anthropic PBC has lined up banks to lead work on its IPO, according to people familiar with the matter, as the artificial intelligence company races its rival OpenAI to be first to go public.

item.title: Warsh’s Fed Debut a Key Risk for FX Markets, Morgan Stanley Says
item.description: Federal Reserve Chairman Kevin Warsh’s first policy meeting this month could potentially jolt foreign-exchange markets and upend consensus carry trades, according to Morgan Stanley.

item.title: Fed Eyes Bank Loans, Nonbanks’ Private Credit Defaults
item.description: US banks’ loan portfolios saw a modest rise in delinquencies last year, a Federal Reserve report showed.

item.title: Goldman’s Minnis Sees AI as ‘Generational’ Force Driving Markets
item.description: The boom in artificial intelligence investment is a “fundamental, generational” phenomenon driving markets and filtering through to the economy at large, said Christina Minnis, global head of the alternatives origination group at Goldman Sachs Group Inc.

item.title: Bill Ackman’s Pershing Square to Exit Universal Music Stake
item.description: Bill Ackman is looking to sell his stake in Universal Music Group NV just days after the Amsterdam-listed company rejected a takeover bid by the hedge fund billionaire.

item.title: Applied Aerospace Shares Rise 3.75% After $650 Million US IPO
item.description: Shares of Applied Aerospace & Defense Inc. rose about 3.75% in its trading debut, after the space and defense engineering firm raised $650 million in an initial public offering priced near the top of a marketed range.

item.title: Barclays’ Wei Says ‘Prepare for War’ in Software Refinancing
item.description: Tremors in the software debt market were still top of mind for industry heavyweights speaking at the Bloomberg Global Credit Forum in New York on Wednesday.

item.title: George Santos’ Kalshi Bets About Himself Spark Insider Trading Investigation
item.description: George Santos has emerged as the latest flashpoint in the controversy over possible insider trading on prediction markets, as concerns become increasingly widespread about potential illicit bets on the platforms.

item.title: Big New BP Shareholder Adds to Stock Despite Boardroom Chaos
item.description: A large new investor in BP Plc topped up its holding after the shock firing of the oil major’s chairman last week, betting that the boardroom drama won’t derail the company’s strategic approach.

item.title: UK Labour Party Warned Against Tacking Left on Wealth Sharing
item.description: UK Business Secretary Peter Kyle warned his Labour colleagues against pursuing redistribution of wealth without first prioritizing economic growth, as the party faces a potential tilt to the left if Keir Starmer is replaced as prime minister.

item.title: JPMorgan to Sell $1.85 Billion Debt for Belden’s Ruckus Buyout
item.description: JPMorgan Chase & Co. is preparing to sell a $1.85 billion debt financing backing Belden’s acquisition of Ruckus Network.

item.title: Lila Sciences Said in Talks for Funds at $8.5 Billion Valuation
item.description: Artificial intelligence research startup Lila Sciences Inc. is in talks to raise about $2 billion at an expected valuation of about $8.5 billion before the new money, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: What Can Nvidia Do to Get Investors Excited Again?
item.description: The only thing harder than creating the world’s most valuable company? Staying on top.

item.title: Vitol Warns Gasoline Could Be Next Fuel to Face Supply Crunch
item.description: Gasoline could be the next petroleum product to come under intense supply pressure as the Iran war rumbles on, according to oil trading giant Vitol Group.

item.title: Vanguard’s VOO Hits $1 Trillion of Assets in ETF Industry First
item.description: A seemingly endless appetite for buying US stock dips has propelled Vanguard Group’s S&P 500-tracking ETF past $1 trillion in assets, making it the first fund of its kind to reach a milestone once thought unimaginable for the ETF industry.

item.title: Dalio Sees AI Bubble Bursting as Wealth Is Converted Into Money
item.description: Billionaire investor Ray Dalio said the booming artificial-intelligence market shows signs of a bubble that will eventually burst.

item.title: Ares Owed $547 Million After Collapse of Textor’s Eagle Football
item.description: Ares Management Corp. was owed more than $547 million following the collapse of John Textor’s Eagle Football Group, according to a filing by the administrators on Wednesday.

item.title: Polish Rates Are ‘High Enough’ as Glapinski Cools Rate Hike Bets
item.description: Poland’s central bank views current interest rate levels as “high enough” to protect price stability amid a spike in global energy costs that’s gradually feeding domestic inflation, Governor Adam Glapinski said.

item.title: Inotiv, Breeder of Laboratory Animals, Files For Bankruptcy
item.description: Inotiv Inc., which breeds dogs and imports monkeys that are used in drug experiments, filed for bankruptcy in part on what it blamed are tighter rules on its US canine farm and tariffs on non-human primates raised in China.

item.title: Musk’s xAI Pauses Hiring for Specialists to Train Grok Chatbot
item.description: Elon Musk’s xAI has paused hiring for professionals to train its Grok chatbot on a range of specialized skills, according to people with knowledge of the matter, marking a potential shift in how the artificial intelligence firm develops its technology.

item.title: Credit Titans Warn of Shakeout on Deals That ‘Don’t Make Sense’
item.description: Beneath the surface of buoyant credit markets, a shakeout is building for leveraged deals made years ago, as well as for direct-lending funds that are grappling with an extended period of investor withdrawals.

item.title: Adobe Eyes Two Internal Leaders, AI Outsiders for CEO Role
item.description: Adobe Inc. is eyeing two internal leaders in its quest for a new chief executive officer and has hired a well-known search firm to find external candidates who could be suited to running the company in the AI era.

item.title: NatWest Names Roose Interim Head of Primary Capital Markets
item.description: NatWest Group Plc has appointed Scott Roose as interim head of its primary capital markets business in a leadership change at the unit.
